subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A mechanical ventilation system, including one that is part of an air-conditioning system, except where the mechanical system serves only a Class 4 part of a building, must be capable of being deactivated when the building or part of the building served by that system is not occupied.

subclause.title: NSW REPLACE SubClause
subclause.p: A mechanical ventilation system, including one that is part of an air-conditioning system, must be capable of being deactivated when the building or part of the building served by that system is not occupied.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: When serving a conditioned space, except in periods when evaporative cooling is being used, a mechanical ventilation system must—

ol.li:
where specified in Table J6D4, have—
an energy reclaiming system that—
preconditions outdoor air at a minimum sensible heat transfer effectiveness of 60%; and
is capable of allowing outdoor air to bypass the heat reclaiming system when the conditions do not provide a heat reclaim benefit; or
indirect evaporative cooling that—
incorporates an energy reclaiming system meeting the requirements of (i); and
has a minimum humidification efficiency of 85%; or
demand control ventilation in accordance with AS 1668.2 if appropriate to the application; and

not exceed the minimum outdoor air quantity required by Part F6 by more than 20%, except—
when additional unconditioned outdoor air is supplied for free cooling; or
where additional mechanical ventilation is needed to balance the required exhaust or process exhaust; or
where an energy reclaiming system meeting the requirements of (2)(a)(i) preconditions all the outdoor air.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: An exhaust system with an air flow rate of more than 1000 L/s must be capable of stopping the motor when the system is not needed, except for an exhaust system in a sole-occupancy unit in a Class 3 or 9c building.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A time switch must be provided to a mechanical ventilation system with an air flow rate of more than 1000 L/s.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The time switch provided under (4) must—

ol.li: be capable of switching electric power on and off at variable pre-programmed times and on variable pre-programmed days; and
have the ability to program public holidays at least 1 year ahead; and

be capable of being overridden by—
a demand-operated control; or
a manual switch; and
allow the resumption of normal operation no longer than 2 hours after any override is activated.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The requirements of (2), (4) and (5) do not apply to a mechanical ventilation system that serves—

ol.li: only one sole-occupancy unit in a Class 3 or 9c building; or
only a Class 4 part of a building.

subclause.title: NSW REPLACE SubClause
subclause.p: The requirements of (2), (4) and (5) do not apply to a mechanical ventilation system that serves only one sole-occupancy unit in a Class 3 or 9c building.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The requirements of (4) and (5) do not apply to—

ol.li: a building where mechanical ventilation is needed for 24 hour occupancy; or
a mechanical ventilation system that is required to operate 24/7 to manage air contaminants, maintain pressurisation or the like.
